---
title: "EDA Prac for lab" 
format: html
---

# Importing libraries 

```{python}
import pandas as pd 
import numpy as np 
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt 
import seaborn as sns 
```

#Load data 
```{python}
budget = pd.read_scv("Maryland_Operating_Budget__FY2024.csv")
budget.columns
budget.head()

```

-Narrow down the columns, excluding the "code" columns, etc.

```{python}
columns_to_keep = ['Fiscal.Year','Agency.Name']
budget = budeget[columns_to_keep]
budget.head()
budget.describe()
len(budget)
```

```{python}
sns.histplot(budget, x = "Budget")
budget["log_dollars"] = np.log10(budget["Budget"])
budget["log_dollars"].isna().sum()
```

#Decide what to do with -$ amounts 

- TBD

# Split up data 

```{python}
budget_zeros = budget[budget["Budget"]== 0]
len(budget_zeros)
budget_neg = budget[budget["Budget"] < 0]
len(budget_neg)
budget_pos = budget[budget["Budget"] > 0]
len(budget_pos)

```

#Start plotting

```{python}
sns.histplot(budget_pos, x = "log_dollars")


```

# Group by 

```{python}

budget_by_agency = budget[["Agency.Name","Budget"]].groupby("Agency.Name").sum()
().sort_values(by = "Budget", ascending = False)


```

##plot by group 

```{python}
selected_agencies = ["Maryland Department of Health" ,  "State Department of Education" , "Department of Trnasportation"]


budget_by_small_agency = budget[budget["Agency.Name"].isin(selected_agencies)].groupby("Agency.Name")


for name, group in budget_by_agency: 
    plt.figure()
    sns.histplot(group, x = "log_dollars").set_title(name)
    set_title(name)
    

```
